content.thinking:
Perfect! I found the issue. The installation script only copied `common.sh` but didn't copy the other 9 library files. The source has 10 files total (common.sh + 9 namespace files) but only common.sh was installed.

This is a bug in the installation script. Let me check the install script to see what went wrong.

content.text: There's your problem! The installation only copied `common.sh` but none of the other 9 namespace library files. Let me check the install script to see what went wrong:

    60→        INSTALL_USER=true

    83→                ;;

    86→                exit 0

   102→
   103→# Show help
   104→show_help() {
   105→    cat <<EOF
   106→Sidekick Installation Script
   107→
   108→Usage:
   109→  install.sh [OPTIONS]
   110→
   111→Options:
   112→  --user              Install to ~/.claude only
   113→  --project           Install to project .claude only
   114→  --both              Install to both scopes (default)
   115→  --features <list>   Install specific features only (comma-separated)
   116→  -h, --help          Show this help message

   119→  install.sh --user
   120→  install.sh --project
   121→  install.sh --both
   122→  install.sh --user --features topic-extraction,resume
   123→
   124→EOF
   125→}
   126→
   127→# Check prerequisites
   128→check_prerequisites() {
   129→    log_step "Checking prerequisites..."
   130→
   131→    # Check if jq is installed
   132→    if ! command -v jq &> /dev/null; then
   133→        log_error "jq is required but not installed. Please install jq first."
   134→        exit 1
   135→    fi
   136→
   137→    # Check if source directory exists
   138→    if [ ! -d "$SRC_DIR" ]; then
   139→        log_error "Source directory not found: $SRC_DIR"
   140→        exit 1
   141→    fi
   142→
   143→    log_info "Prerequisites check passed"
   144→}
   145→
   146→# Copy files to destination
   147→copy_files() {
   148→    local dest_dir="$1"
   149→
   150→    log_step "Copying files to $dest_dir..."
   151→
   152→    # Create directory structure
   153→    mkdir -p "$dest_dir"
   154→    mkdir -p "$dest_dir/lib"
   155→    mkdir -p "$dest_dir/handlers"
   156→    mkdir -p "$dest_dir/features"
   157→    mkdir -p "$dest_dir/features/prompts"
   158→    mkdir -p "$dest_dir/config"
   159→
   160→    # Copy main entry point
   161→    cp "$SRC_DIR/sidekick.sh" "$dest_dir/"
   162→    chmod +x "$dest_dir/sidekick.sh"
   163→
   164→    # Copy library
   165→    cp "$SRC_DIR/lib/common.sh" "$dest_dir/lib/"
   166→
   167→    # Copy handlers
   168→    cp "$SRC_DIR/handlers"/*.sh "$dest_dir/handlers/"
   169→
   170→    # Copy features (all or selected)
   171→    if [ -n "$SELECTED_FEATURES" ]; then
   172→        # Copy selected features
   173→        IFS=',' read -ra FEATURES <<< "$SELECTED_FEATURES"
   174→        for feature in "${FEATURES[@]}"; do
   175→            if [ -f "$SRC_DIR/features/${feature}.sh" ]; then
   176→                cp "$SRC_DIR/features/${feature}.sh" "$dest_dir/features/"
   177→            else
   178→                log_warn "Feature not found: ${feature}.sh"
   179→            fi
   180→        done
   181→    else
   182→        # Copy all features
   183→        cp "$SRC_DIR/features"/*.sh "$dest_dir/features/"
   184→    fi
   185→
   186→    # Copy prompts
   187→    cp "$SRC_DIR/features/prompts"/*.txt "$dest_dir/features/prompts/"
   188→
   189→    # Copy config defaults
   190→    cp "$SRC_DIR/config.defaults" "$dest_dir/"
   191→
   192→    # Copy static reminder if it exists
   193→    if [ -f "$SRC_DIR/config/static-reminder.txt" ]; then
   194→        cp "$SRC_DIR/config/static-reminder.txt" "$dest_dir/config/"
   195→    fi
   196→
   197→    # Create sidekick.conf if it doesn't exist (don't overwrite)
   198→    if [ ! -f "$dest_dir/sidekick.conf" ]; then
   199→        cp "$SRC_DIR/config.defaults" "$dest_dir/sidekick.conf"
   200→        log_info "Created sidekick.conf from defaults"
   201→    else
   202→        log_info "Preserving existing sidekick.conf"
   203→    fi
   204→
   205→    log_info "Files copied successfully"
   206→}
   207→
   208→# Register hooks in settings.json
   209→register_hooks_in_settings() {
   210→    local settings_file="$1"
   211→    local sidekick_path="$2"
   212→
   213→    log_step "Registering hooks in $settings_file..."
   214→
   215→    # Create backup of existing settings
   216→    if [ -f "$settings_file" ]; then
   217→        local backup_file="${settings_file}.backup.$(date +%Y%m%d-%H%M%S)"
   218→        cp "$settings_file" "$backup_file"
   219→        log_info "Backup created: $backup_file"
   220→    fi
   221→
   222→    # Create .claude directory if it doesn't exist
   223→    mkdir -p "$(dirname "$settings_file")"
   224→
   225→    # Read existing settings or create empty object
   226→    local settings="{}"
   227→    if [ -f "$settings_file" ]; then
   228→        settings=$(cat "$settings_file")
   229→    fi
   230→
   231→    # Add hooks using jq
   232→    settings=$(echo "$settings" | jq \
   233→        --arg session_cmd "${sidekick_path}/sidekick.sh session-start \"\$CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR\"" \
   234→        --arg prompt_cmd "${sidekick_path}/sidekick.sh user-prompt-submit \"\$CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR\"" \
   235→        --arg status_cmd "${sidekick_path}/sidekick.sh statusline --project-dir \"\$CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR\"" \
   236→        '
   237→        .hooks.SessionStart = [{
   238→            "hooks": [{
   239→                "type": "command",
   240→                "command": $session_cmd
   241→            }]
   242→        }] |
   243→        .hooks.UserPromptSubmit = [{
   244→            "hooks": [{
   245→                "type": "command",
   246→                "command": $prompt_cmd
   247→            }]
   248→        }] |
   249→        .statusLine = {
   250→            "type": "command",
   251→            "command": $status_cmd
   252→        }
   253→        ')
   254→
   255→    # Write updated settings
   256→    echo "$settings" | jq '.' > "$settings_file"
   257→
   258→    log_info "Hooks registered successfully"
   259→}
   260→
   261→# Update .claudeignore
   262→update_claudeignore() {
   263→    local project_dir="$1"
   264→    local ignore_file="$project_dir/.claudeignore"
   265→
   266→    log_step "Updating .claudeignore..."
   267→
   268→    # Create .claudeignore if it doesn't exist
   269→    if [ ! -f "$ignore_file" ]; then
   270→        touch "$ignore_file"
   271→    fi
   272→
   273→    # Add sidekick session state directory if not already present
   274→    if ! grep -q "^\.sidekick/" "$ignore_file" 2>/dev/null; then
   275→        echo ".sidekick/" >> "$ignore_file"
   276→        log_info "Added .sidekick/ to .claudeignore"
   277→    else
   278→        log_info ".claudeignore already contains .sidekick/ directory"
   279→    fi
   280→}
   281→
   282→# Install to user scope
   283→install_to_user() {
   284→    log_info "Installing to user scope (~/.claude)..."
   285→
   286→    local user_dir="$HOME/.claude/hooks/sidekick"
   287→    local settings_file="$HOME/.claude/settings.json"
   288→
   289→    # Copy files
   290→    copy_files "$user_dir"
   291→
   292→    # Register hooks
   293→    register_hooks_in_settings "$settings_file" "~/.claude/hooks/sidekick"
   294→
   295→    log_info "User scope installation complete"
   296→}
   297→
   298→# Install to project scope
   299→install_to_project() {
   300→    log_info "Installing to project scope (.claude)..."
   301→
   302→    # Determine project directory (current directory if in a project)
   303→    local project_dir="${CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR:-$PWD}"
   304→    local project_sidekick_dir="$project_dir/.claude/hooks/sidekick"
   305→    local settings_file="$project_dir/.claude/settings.json"
   306→
   307→    # Copy files
   308→    copy_files "$project_sidekick_dir"
   309→
   310→    # Register hooks (use $CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR variable in paths)
   311→    register_hooks_in_settings "$settings_file" "\$CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR/.claude/hooks/sidekick"
   312→
   313→    # Update .claudeignore
   314→    update_claudeignore "$project_dir"
   315→
   316→    log_info "Project scope installation complete"
   317→}
   318→
   319→# Main installation flow
   320→main() {
   321→    echo ""
   322→    echo "=================================================="
   323→    echo "  Sidekick Installation"
   324→    echo "=================================================="
   325→    echo ""
   326→
   327→    # Parse arguments
   328→    parse_args "$@"
   329→
   330→    # Check prerequisites
   331→    check_prerequisites
   332→
   333→    # Show installation plan
   334→    echo ""
   335→    log_info "Installation plan:"
   336→    if [ "$INSTALL_USER" = true ]; then
   337→        echo "  - User scope: ~/.claude/hooks/sidekick"
   338→    fi
   339→    if [ "$INSTALL_PROJECT" = true ]; then
   340→        echo "  - Project scope: .claude/hooks/sidekick"
   341→    fi
   342→    if [ -n "$SELECTED_FEATURES" ]; then
   343→        echo "  - Features: $SELECTED_FEATURES"
   344→    else
   345→        echo "  - Features: all"
   346→    fi
   347→    echo ""
   348→
   349→    # Install to user scope
   350→    if [ "$INSTALL_USER" = true ]; then
   351→        install_to_user
   352→        echo ""
   353→    fi
   354→
   355→    # Install to project scope
   356→    if [ "$INSTALL_PROJECT" = true ]; then
   357→        install_to_project
   358→        echo ""
   359→    fi
   360→
   361→    # Success
   362→    echo "=================================================="
   363→    log_info "Installation complete!"
   364→    echo "=================================================="
   365→    echo ""
   366→    echo "Next steps:"
   367→    echo "  1. Review configuration: cat ~/.claude/hooks/sidekick/sidekick.conf"
   368→    echo "  2. Start a new Claude session to activate hooks"
   369→    echo "  3. Logs will be created in .sidekick/sessions/\${session_id}/sidekick.log on first use"
   370→    echo ""
   371→}
   372→
   373→# Run main
   374→main "$@"
   375→

file.content:
#!/bin/bash
# install.sh - Install Sidekick to user and/or project scope
#
# Usage:
#   install.sh [--user|--project|--both] [--features <list>]
#
# Options:
#   --user              Install to ~/.claude only
#   --project           Install to project .claude only
#   --both              Install to both (default)
#   --features <list>   Install specific features only (comma-separated)
#
# Examples:
#   install.sh --user
#   install.sh --project
#   install.sh --both
#   install.sh --user --features topic-extraction,resume

set -euo pipefail

SCRIPT_DIR="$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)"
PROJECT_ROOT="$(cd "$SCRIPT_DIR/.." && pwd)"
SRC_DIR="$PROJECT_ROOT/src/sidekick"

# ANSI colors
RED='\033[0;31m'
GREEN='\033[0;32m'
YELLOW='\033[1;33m'
BLUE='\033[0;34m'
NC='\033[0m' # No Color

# Installation scope
INSTALL_USER=false
INSTALL_PROJECT=false

# Feature selection (empty = all)
SELECTED_FEATURES=""

# Logging functions
log_info() {
    echo -e "${GREEN}[INFO]${NC} $*"
}

log_warn() {
    echo -e "${YELLOW}[WARN]${NC} $*"
}

log_error() {
    echo -e "${RED}[ERROR]${NC} $*"
}

log_step() {
    echo -e "${BLUE}[STEP]${NC} $*"
}

# Parse command-line arguments
parse_args() {
    if [ $# -eq 0 ]; then
        # Default to --both if no args
        INSTALL_USER=true
        INSTALL_PROJECT=true
        return
    fi

    while [ $# -gt 0 ]; do
        case "$1" in
            --user)
                INSTALL_USER=true
                shift
                ;;
            --project)
                INSTALL_PROJECT=true
                shift
                ;;
            --both)
                INSTALL_USER=true
                INSTALL_PROJECT=true
                shift
                ;;
            --features)
                SELECTED_FEATURES="$2"
                shift 2
                ;;
            -h|--help)
                show_help
                exit 0
                ;;
            *)
                log_error "Unknown option: $1"
                show_help
                exit 1
                ;;
        esac
    done

    # If neither specified, default to both
    if [ "$INSTALL_USER" = false ] && [ "$INSTALL_PROJECT" = false ]; then
        INSTALL_USER=true
        INSTALL_PROJECT=true
    fi
}

# Show help
show_help() {
    cat <<EOF
Sidekick Installation Script

Usage:
  install.sh [OPTIONS]

Options:
  --user              Install to ~/.claude only
  --project           Install to project .claude only
  --both              Install to both scopes (default)
  --features <list>   Install specific features only (comma-separated)
  -h, --help          Show this help message

Examples:
  install.sh --user
  install.sh --project
  install.sh --both
  install.sh --user --features topic-extraction,resume

EOF
}

# Check prerequisites
check_prerequisites() {
    log_step "Checking prerequisites..."

    # Check if jq is installed
    if ! command -v jq &> /dev/null; then
        log_error "jq is required but not installed. Please install jq first."
        exit 1
    fi

    # Check if source directory exists
    if [ ! -d "$SRC_DIR" ]; then
        log_error "Source directory not found: $SRC_DIR"
        exit 1
    fi

    log_info "Prerequisites check passed"
}

# Copy files to destination
copy_files() {
    local dest_dir="$1"

    log_step "Copying files to $dest_dir..."

    # Create directory structure
    mkdir -p "$dest_dir"
    mkdir -p "$dest_dir/lib"
    mkdir -p "$dest_dir/handlers"
    mkdir -p "$dest_dir/features"
    mkdir -p "$dest_dir/features/prompts"
    mkdir -p "$dest_dir/config"

    # Copy main entry point
    cp "$SRC_DIR/sidekick.sh" "$dest_dir/"
    chmod +x "$dest_dir/sidekick.sh"

    # Copy library
    cp "$SRC_DIR/lib/common.sh" "$dest_dir/lib/"

    # Copy handlers
    cp "$SRC_DIR/handlers"/*.sh "$dest_dir/handlers/"

    # Copy features (all or selected)
    if [ -n "$SELECTED_FEATURES" ]; then
        # Copy selected features
        IFS=',' read -ra FEATURES <<< "$SELECTED_FEATURES"
        for feature in "${FEATURES[@]}"; do
            if [ -f "$SRC_DIR/features/${feature}.sh" ]; then
                cp "$SRC_DIR/features/${feature}.sh" "$dest_dir/features/"
            else
                log_warn "Feature not found: ${feature}.sh"
            fi
        done
    else
        # Copy all features
        cp "$SRC_DIR/features"/*.sh "$dest_dir/features/"
    fi

    # Copy prompts
    cp "$SRC_DIR/features/prompts"/*.txt "$dest_dir/features/prompts/"

    # Copy config defaults
    cp "$SRC_DIR/config.defaults" "$dest_dir/"

    # Copy static reminder if it exists
    if [ -f "$SRC_DIR/config/static-reminder.txt" ]; then
        cp "$SRC_DIR/config/static-reminder.txt" "$dest_dir/config/"
    fi

    # Create sidekick.conf if it doesn't exist (don't overwrite)
    if [ ! -f "$dest_dir/sidekick.conf" ]; then
        cp "$SRC_DIR/config.defaults" "$dest_dir/sidekick.conf"
        log_info "Created sidekick.conf from defaults"
    else
        log_info "Preserving existing sidekick.conf"
    fi

    log_info "Files copied successfully"
}

# Register hooks in settings.json
register_hooks_in_settings() {
    local settings_file="$1"
    local sidekick_path="$2"

    log_step "Registering hooks in $settings_file..."

    # Create backup of existing settings
    if [ -f "$settings_file" ]; then
        local backup_file="${settings_file}.backup.$(date +%Y%m%d-%H%M%S)"
        cp "$settings_file" "$backup_file"
        log_info "Backup created: $backup_file"
    fi

    # Create .claude directory if it doesn't exist
    mkdir -p "$(dirname "$settings_file")"

    # Read existing settings or create empty object
    local settings="{}"
    if [ -f "$settings_file" ]; then
        settings=$(cat "$settings_file")
    fi

    # Add hooks using jq
    settings=$(echo "$settings" | jq \
        --arg session_cmd "${sidekick_path}/sidekick.sh session-start \"\$CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR\"" \
        --arg prompt_cmd "${sidekick_path}/sidekick.sh user-prompt-submit \"\$CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR\"" \
        --arg status_cmd "${sidekick_path}/sidekick.sh statusline --project-dir \"\$CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR\"" \
        '
        .hooks.SessionStart = [{
            "hooks": [{
                "type": "command",
                "command": $session_cmd
            }]
        }] |
        .hooks.UserPromptSubmit = [{
            "hooks": [{
                "type": "command",
                "command": $prompt_cmd
            }]
        }] |
        .statusLine = {
            "type": "command",
            "command": $status_cmd
        }
        ')

    # Write updated settings
    echo "$settings" | jq '.' > "$settings_file"

    log_info "Hooks registered successfully"
}

# Update .claudeignore
update_claudeignore() {
    local project_dir="$1"
    local ignore_file="$project_dir/.claudeignore"

    log_step "Updating .claudeignore..."

    # Create .claudeignore if it doesn't exist
    if [ ! -f "$ignore_file" ]; then
        touch "$ignore_file"
    fi

    # Add sidekick session state directory if not already present
    if ! grep -q "^\.sidekick/" "$ignore_file" 2>/dev/null; then
        echo ".sidekick/" >> "$ignore_file"
        log_info "Added .sidekick/ to .claudeignore"
    else
        log_info ".claudeignore already contains .sidekick/ directory"
    fi
}

# Install to user scope
install_to_user() {
    log_info "Installing to user scope (~/.claude)..."

    local user_dir="$HOME/.claude/hooks/sidekick"
    local settings_file="$HOME/.claude/settings.json"

    # Copy files
    copy_files "$user_dir"

    # Register hooks
    register_hooks_in_settings "$settings_file" "~/.claude/hooks/sidekick"

    log_info "User scope installation complete"
}

# Install to project scope
install_to_project() {
    log_info "Installing to project scope (.claude)..."

    # Determine project directory (current directory if in a project)
    local project_dir="${CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR:-$PWD}"
    local project_sidekick_dir="$project_dir/.claude/hooks/sidekick"
    local settings_file="$project_dir/.claude/settings.json"

    # Copy files
    copy_files "$project_sidekick_dir"

    # Register hooks (use $CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR variable in paths)
    register_hooks_in_settings "$settings_file" "\$CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR/.claude/hooks/sidekick"

    # Update .claudeignore
    update_claudeignore "$project_dir"

    log_info "Project scope installation complete"
}

# Main installation flow
main() {
    echo ""
    echo "=================================================="
    echo "  Sidekick Installation"
    echo "=================================================="
    echo ""

    # Parse arguments
    parse_args "$@"

    # Check prerequisites
    check_prerequisites

    # Show installation plan
    echo ""
    log_info "Installation plan:"
    if [ "$INSTALL_USER" = true ]; then
        echo "  - User scope: ~/.claude/hooks/sidekick"
    fi
    if [ "$INSTALL_PROJECT" = true ]; then
        echo "  - Project scope: .claude/hooks/sidekick"
    fi
    if [ -n "$SELECTED_FEATURES" ]; then
        echo "  - Features: $SELECTED_FEATURES"
    else
        echo "  - Features: all"
    fi
    echo ""

    # Install to user scope
    if [ "$INSTALL_USER" = true ]; then
        install_to_user
        echo ""
    fi

    # Install to project scope
    if [ "$INSTALL_PROJECT" = true ]; then
        install_to_project
        echo ""
    fi

    # Success
    echo "=================================================="
    log_info "Installation complete!"
    echo "=================================================="
    echo ""
    echo "Next steps:"
    echo "  1. Review configuration: cat ~/.claude/hooks/sidekick/sidekick.conf"
    echo "  2. Start a new Claude session to activate hooks"
    echo "  3. Logs will be created in .sidekick/sessions/\${session_id}/sidekick.log on first use"
    echo ""
}

# Run main
main "$@"

content.thinking:
Found the bug! On line 165, the install script only copies `common.sh`:

```bash
# Copy library
cp "$SRC_DIR/lib/common.sh" "$dest_dir/lib/"
```

But it should copy ALL the lib files. According to the architecture, there are 10 files in total:
- common.sh (loader)
- config.sh
- json.sh
- llm.sh
- logging.sh
- paths.sh
- plugin.sh
- process.sh
- utils.sh
- workspace.sh

The fix is simple - change line 165 to copy all .sh files from lib/.

content.text:

```bash
# Copy library
cp "$SRC_DIR/lib/common.sh" "$dest_dir/lib/"
```

This should copy ALL the lib files. Let me fix it:

toolUseResult.originalFile:
#!/bin/bash
# install.sh - Install Sidekick to user and/or project scope
#
# Usage:
#   install.sh [--user|--project|--both] [--features <list>]
#
# Options:
#   --user              Install to ~/.claude only
#   --project           Install to project .claude only
#   --both              Install to both (default)
#   --features <list>   Install specific features only (comma-separated)
#
# Examples:
#   install.sh --user
#   install.sh --project
#   install.sh --both
#   install.sh --user --features topic-extraction,resume

set -euo pipefail

SCRIPT_DIR="$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)"
PROJECT_ROOT="$(cd "$SCRIPT_DIR/.." && pwd)"
SRC_DIR="$PROJECT_ROOT/src/sidekick"

# ANSI colors
RED='\033[0;31m'
GREEN='\033[0;32m'
YELLOW='\033[1;33m'
BLUE='\033[0;34m'
NC='\033[0m' # No Color

# Installation scope
INSTALL_USER=false
INSTALL_PROJECT=false

# Feature selection (empty = all)
SELECTED_FEATURES=""

# Logging functions
log_info() {
    echo -e "${GREEN}[INFO]${NC} $*"
}

log_warn() {
    echo -e "${YELLOW}[WARN]${NC} $*"
}

log_error() {
    echo -e "${RED}[ERROR]${NC} $*"
}

log_step() {
    echo -e "${BLUE}[STEP]${NC} $*"
}

# Parse command-line arguments
parse_args() {
    if [ $# -eq 0 ]; then
        # Default to --both if no args
        INSTALL_USER=true
        INSTALL_PROJECT=true
        return
    fi

    while [ $# -gt 0 ]; do
        case "$1" in
            --user)
                INSTALL_USER=true
                shift
                ;;
            --project)
                INSTALL_PROJECT=true
                shift
                ;;
            --both)
                INSTALL_USER=true
                INSTALL_PROJECT=true
                shift
                ;;
            --features)
                SELECTED_FEATURES="$2"
                shift 2
                ;;
            -h|--help)
                show_help
                exit 0
                ;;
            *)
                log_error "Unknown option: $1"
                show_help
                exit 1
                ;;
        esac
    done

    # If neither specified, default to both
    if [ "$INSTALL_USER" = false ] && [ "$INSTALL_PROJECT" = false ]; then
        INSTALL_USER=true
        INSTALL_PROJECT=true
    fi
}

# Show help
show_help() {
    cat <<EOF
Sidekick Installation Script

Usage:
  install.sh [OPTIONS]

Options:
  --user              Install to ~/.claude only
  --project           Install to project .claude only
  --both              Install to both scopes (default)
  --features <list>   Install specific features only (comma-separated)
  -h, --help          Show this help message

Examples:
  install.sh --user
  install.sh --project
  install.sh --both
  install.sh --user --features topic-extraction,resume

EOF
}

# Check prerequisites
check_prerequisites() {
    log_step "Checking prerequisites..."

    # Check if jq is installed
    if ! command -v jq &> /dev/null; then
        log_error "jq is required but not installed. Please install jq first."
        exit 1
    fi

    # Check if source directory exists
    if [ ! -d "$SRC_DIR" ]; then
        log_error "Source directory not found: $SRC_DIR"
        exit 1
    fi

    log_info "Prerequisites check passed"
}

# Copy files to destination
copy_files() {
    local dest_dir="$1"

    log_step "Copying files to $dest_dir..."

    # Create directory structure
    mkdir -p "$dest_dir"
    mkdir -p "$dest_dir/lib"
    mkdir -p "$dest_dir/handlers"
    mkdir -p "$dest_dir/features"
    mkdir -p "$dest_dir/features/prompts"
    mkdir -p "$dest_dir/config"

    # Copy main entry point
    cp "$SRC_DIR/sidekick.sh" "$dest_dir/"
    chmod +x "$dest_dir/sidekick.sh"

    # Copy library
    cp "$SRC_DIR/lib/common.sh" "$dest_dir/lib/"

    # Copy handlers
    cp "$SRC_DIR/handlers"/*.sh "$dest_dir/handlers/"

    # Copy features (all or selected)
    if [ -n "$SELECTED_FEATURES" ]; then
        # Copy selected features
        IFS=',' read -ra FEATURES <<< "$SELECTED_FEATURES"
        for feature in "${FEATURES[@]}"; do
            if [ -f "$SRC_DIR/features/${feature}.sh" ]; then
                cp "$SRC_DIR/features/${feature}.sh" "$dest_dir/features/"
            else
                log_warn "Feature not found: ${feature}.sh"
            fi
        done
    else
        # Copy all features
        cp "$SRC_DIR/features"/*.sh "$dest_dir/features/"
    fi

    # Copy prompts
    cp "$SRC_DIR/features/prompts"/*.txt "$dest_dir/features/prompts/"

    # Copy config defaults
    cp "$SRC_DIR/config.defaults" "$dest_dir/"

    # Copy static reminder if it exists
    if [ -f "$SRC_DIR/config/static-reminder.txt" ]; then
        cp "$SRC_DIR/config/static-reminder.txt" "$dest_dir/config/"
    fi

    # Create sidekick.conf if it doesn't exist (don't overwrite)
    if [ ! -f "$dest_dir/sidekick.conf" ]; then
        cp "$SRC_DIR/config.defaults" "$dest_dir/sidekick.conf"
        log_info "Created sidekick.conf from defaults"
    else
        log_info "Preserving existing sidekick.conf"
    fi

    log_info "Files copied successfully"
}

# Register hooks in settings.json
register_hooks_in_settings() {
    local settings_file="$1"
    local sidekick_path="$2"

    log_step "Registering hooks in $settings_file..."

    # Create backup of existing settings
    if [ -f "$settings_file" ]; then
        local backup_file="${settings_file}.backup.$(date +%Y%m%d-%H%M%S)"
        cp "$settings_file" "$backup_file"
        log_info "Backup created: $backup_file"
    fi

    # Create .claude directory if it doesn't exist
    mkdir -p "$(dirname "$settings_file")"

    # Read existing settings or create empty object
    local settings="{}"
    if [ -f "$settings_file" ]; then
        settings=$(cat "$settings_file")
    fi

    # Add hooks using jq
    settings=$(echo "$settings" | jq \
        --arg session_cmd "${sidekick_path}/sidekick.sh session-start \"\$CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR\"" \
        --arg prompt_cmd "${sidekick_path}/sidekick.sh user-prompt-submit \"\$CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR\"" \
        --arg status_cmd "${sidekick_path}/sidekick.sh statusline --project-dir \"\$CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR\"" \
        '
        .hooks.SessionStart = [{
            "hooks": [{
                "type": "command",
                "command": $session_cmd
            }]
        }] |
        .hooks.UserPromptSubmit = [{
            "hooks": [{
                "type": "command",
                "command": $prompt_cmd
            }]
        }] |
        .statusLine = {
            "type": "command",
            "command": $status_cmd
        }
        ')

    # Write updated settings
    echo "$settings" | jq '.' > "$settings_file"

    log_info "Hooks registered successfully"
}

# Update .claudeignore
update_claudeignore() {
    local project_dir="$1"
    local ignore_file="$project_dir/.claudeignore"

    log_step "Updating .claudeignore..."

    # Create .claudeignore if it doesn't exist
    if [ ! -f "$ignore_file" ]; then
        touch "$ignore_file"
    fi

    # Add sidekick session state directory if not already present
    if ! grep -q "^\.sidekick/" "$ignore_file" 2>/dev/null; then
        echo ".sidekick/" >> "$ignore_file"
        log_info "Added .sidekick/ to .claudeignore"
    else
        log_info ".claudeignore already contains .sidekick/ directory"
    fi
}

# Install to user scope
install_to_user() {
    log_info "Installing to user scope (~/.claude)..."

    local user_dir="$HOME/.claude/hooks/sidekick"
    local settings_file="$HOME/.claude/settings.json"

    # Copy files
    copy_files "$user_dir"

    # Register hooks
    register_hooks_in_settings "$settings_file" "~/.claude/hooks/sidekick"

    log_info "User scope installation complete"
}

# Install to project scope
install_to_project() {
    log_info "Installing to project scope (.claude)..."

    # Determine project directory (current directory if in a project)
    local project_dir="${CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR:-$PWD}"
    local project_sidekick_dir="$project_dir/.claude/hooks/sidekick"
    local settings_file="$project_dir/.claude/settings.json"

    # Copy files
    copy_files "$project_sidekick_dir"

    # Register hooks (use $CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR variable in paths)
    register_hooks_in_settings "$settings_file" "\$CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR/.claude/hooks/sidekick"

    # Update .claudeignore
    update_claudeignore "$project_dir"

    log_info "Project scope installation complete"
}

# Main installation flow
main() {
    echo ""
    echo "=================================================="
    echo "  Sidekick Installation"
    echo "=================================================="
    echo ""

    # Parse arguments
    parse_args "$@"

    # Check prerequisites
    check_prerequisites

    # Show installation plan
    echo ""
    log_info "Installation plan:"
    if [ "$INSTALL_USER" = true ]; then
        echo "  - User scope: ~/.claude/hooks/sidekick"
    fi
    if [ "$INSTALL_PROJECT" = true ]; then
        echo "  - Project scope: .claude/hooks/sidekick"
    fi
    if [ -n "$SELECTED_FEATURES" ]; then
        echo "  - Features: $SELECTED_FEATURES"
    else
        echo "  - Features: all"
    fi
    echo ""

    # Install to user scope
    if [ "$INSTALL_USER" = true ]; then
        install_to_user
        echo ""
    fi

    # Install to project scope
    if [ "$INSTALL_PROJECT" = true ]; then
        install_to_project
        echo ""
    fi

    # Success
    echo "=================================================="
    log_info "Installation complete!"
    echo "=================================================="
    echo ""
    echo "Next steps:"
    echo "  1. Review configuration: cat ~/.claude/hooks/sidekick/sidekick.conf"
    echo "  2. Start a new Claude session to activate hooks"
    echo "  3. Logs will be created in .sidekick/sessions/\${session_id}/sidekick.log on first use"
    echo ""
}

# Run main
main "$@"

content.text:
## Fixed!

**The Problem:**
Your install script at `scripts/install.sh:165` was only copying `common.sh` instead of all 10 library files. The modular architecture requires all namespace files:
- common.sh (loader)
- config.sh, json.sh, llm.sh, logging.sh, paths.sh, plugin.sh, process.sh, utils.sh, workspace.sh

**The Fix:**
Changed line 165 from:
```bash
cp "$SRC_DIR/lib/common.sh" "$dest_dir/lib/"
```
to:
```bash
cp "$SRC_DIR/lib"/*.sh "$dest_dir/lib/"
```

**What I Did:**
1. Fixed the install script
2. Re-ran the installation (which preserved your existing `sidekick.conf`)
3. Verified all 10 lib files are now present
4. Tested the session-start hook - it works perfectly now (all 6 plugins loaded)

**Next Step:**
You need to **restart Claude** to pick up the fixed installation:
```bash
claude --continue
```

The session-start hook should now work without errors. The "startup hook error" was because `common.sh` was trying to source `logging.sh` which didn't exist.
